# Phase 10.0.1 Governance Freeze Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, or execution authority.

## Objective

Freeze, preserve, seal, and archive the completed constitutional governance civilization era after closure certification.

## Scope

- Governance freeze enforcement doctrine.
- Immutable constitutional preservation.
- Governance-era sealing doctrine.
- Civilization archive packaging standards.
- Constitutional snapshot lineage.
- Preservation-chain verification.
- Anti-post-closure mutation doctrine.
- Historical authenticity sealing.
- Governance freeze integrity doctrine.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No governance-freeze system creates runtime authority.
- No preservation architecture bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No archive packaging layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No snapshot-lineage framework creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No freeze doctrine weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows Phase 9.2.6 civilization governance era closure certification.
- Safe to include now: freeze enforcement, source preservation, sealing, archive packaging, snapshot lineage, preservation verification, anti-mutation doctrine, authenticity sealing, and integrity doctrine.
- Future upgrade: constitutional archive manifest and doctrine index certification.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, API integrations, background jobs, autonomous governance, or execution systems.
